title: Robata-Grill Roasted Peruvian Pork Chops Tacu Tacu
description: A fun and kid-friendly Peruvian dish featuring pork chops, sautéed onions and rice, and a crispy robata-grill crust. This recipe replaces the traditional Latin American dish with a FODMAP-friendly alternative.

ingredients.name: Uncooked white rice
ingredients.notes: short-grain rice like calrose or Japanese rice is recommended

instructions: Season the pork chops with salt and black pepper on both sides.
Sear the pork chops on the robata grill for 3-4 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked through.
Transfer the pork chops to a plate and set aside.
In the same robata grill, add the olive oil and sauté the onions until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the garlic and sauté for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the uncooked white rice to the robata grill and stir to coat with the oil and mix with the onions and garlic.
Add the chicken broth, soy sauce, and Peruvian aji amarillo pepper sauce to the robata grill, and stir to combine.
Return the pork chops to the robata grill and spoon some of the rice mixture over the top of each chop.
Cover the robata grill with a lid or foil and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot.
Let the pork chops rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
